turns.content: Good afternoon, and welcome to the UnitedHealth Group Inc. Q1 fiscal year 2024 earnings conference call. All lines have been placed on mute to prevent background noise. After the speakers' remarks, there will be a question and answer session. I would now like to turn the call over to the head of investor relations.

I am extremely pleased with our performance this quarter. The momentum we are seeing is unprecedented. In Q1 2024, Our enrollment growth reached an all-time high of $35.5B, driven by strong demand across all regions. Our digital health strategy continues to gain traction, with Optum Health showing particularly strong adoption among enterprise customers. We are investing significantly in digital health because we believe this represents a generational opportunity. The early results from health insurance validate our approach. Looking ahead, we see continued strength in demand and expect to deliver above-trend growth next quarter.

Thank you, Andrew. Let me walk through the financial details. Total revenue for Q1 2024 was $89.2 billion, representing growth of 19% year over year. Our medical care ratio reached $23.4 billion, accelerating the strong demand trends we have been seeing. Gross margin expanded 30.4%, reflecting our continued investment in growth. Operating expenses were well managed, and we generated $8.1 billion in free cash flow during the quarter. We returned $7 billion to shareholders through dividends and share repurchases. For UnitedHealthcare, we saw 14% growth, driven by strong demand fundamentals.
Great, thanks. On Optum Health, can you talk about what you are seeing in terms of customer adoption and how the competitive landscape is evolving?

Sure, Toni. The operating margin trend reflects the underlying strength in our business model. When you look at the unit economics, they continue to improve and that gives us confidence in the sustainability of these results.
My question is on the demand environment. Are you seeing any changes in customer behavior or deal cycles compared to last quarter, particularly in Optum Rx?

Sure, Karl. The enrollment growth trend reflects the underlying strength in our business model. When you look at the unit economics, they continue to improve and that gives us confidence in the sustainability of these results.
Can you help us understand the margin dynamics better? What is driving the expansion and how should we model this going forward?

Thanks, Shannon. Great question. On medical care ratio, we are seeing exceptionally strong demand and our conviction in value-based care has only strengthened. The opportunity ahead of us is significant and we are investing aggressively to capture it. We expect Optum Health to continue to be a meaningful growth driver as we move through the rest of the year. Let me hand it to John for the specific numbers.

Thank you for taking my question. Can you provide more color on the enrollment growth trajectory? Specifically, how should we think about the sustainability of the growth rate you reported this quarter?

Sure, Keith. The enrollment growth trend reflects the underlying strength in our business model. When you look at the unit economics, they continue to improve and that gives us confidence in the sustainability of these results.
This concludes the question and answer session. I would like to turn the call back to management for closing remarks.
Thank you all for joining us today. We are excited about the opportunities ahead and look forward to updating you on our progress next quarter. Have a great evening.

Good afternoon, and welcome to the UnitedHealth Group Inc. Q2 fiscal year 2024 earnings conference call. All lines have been placed on mute to prevent background noise. After the speakers' remarks, there will be a question and answer session. I would now like to turn the call over to the head of investor relations.

This quarter demonstrated resilience across our business despite a mixed macroeconomic environment. In Q2 2024, We achieved 4% growth in Optum Health, which is in the range we expected given current market conditions. Our membership growth strategy continues to gain traction, with Optum Health showing particularly strong adoption among enterprise customers. We are investing significantly in membership growth because we believe this represents a generational opportunity. The early results from data analytics validate our approach. For the upcoming quarter, we expect results roughly in line with seasonal patterns.

Thank you, Andrew. Let me walk through the financial details. Total revenue for Q2 2024 was $105.1 billion, representing growth of 24% year over year. Our medical care ratio reached $48.1 billion, consistent with our expectations. Gross margin expanded 23.5%, reflecting our continued investment in growth. Operating expenses were well managed, and we generated $21.7 billion in free cash flow during the quarter. We returned $2 billion to shareholders through dividends and share repurchases. For UnitedHealthcare, we saw 13% growth, driven by strong demand fundamentals.
I wanted to ask about value-based care. How much of your capital allocation is going toward this area, and when do you expect it to start contributing meaningfully to the top line?

Thanks, Keith. Great question. On enrollment growth, we are pleased with the progress on value-based care and continue to see steady adoption. We are being disciplined in our investments while ensuring we capture the right opportunities. We expect Optum Insight to continue to be a meaningful growth driver as we move through the rest of the year. Let me hand it to John for the specific numbers.
Great, thanks. On Optum Health, can you talk about what you are seeing in terms of customer adoption and how the competitive landscape is evolving?

Thanks, Shannon. Great question. On operating margin, we are pleased with the progress on cost management and continue to see steady adoption. We are being disciplined in our investments while ensuring we capture the right opportunities. We expect Optum Health to continue to be a meaningful growth driver as we move through the rest of the year. Let me hand it to John for the specific numbers.

Thank you for taking my question. Can you provide more color on the enrollment growth trajectory? Specifically, how should we think about the sustainability of the growth rate you reported this quarter?

Sure, Amit. The enrollment growth trend reflects the underlying strength in our business model. When you look at the unit economics, they continue to improve and that gives us confidence in the sustainability of these results.
Can you help us understand the margin dynamics better? What is driving the pressure and how should we model this going forward?

Sure, Raimo. The operating margin trend reflects the underlying strength in our business model. When you look at the unit economics, they continue to improve and that gives us confidence in the sustainability of these results.
This concludes the question and answer session. I would like to turn the call back to management for closing remarks.
Thank you all for joining us today. We are excited about the opportunities ahead and look forward to updating you on our progress next quarter. Have a great evening.

Good afternoon, and welcome to the UnitedHealth Group Inc. Q3 fiscal year 2024 earnings conference call. All lines have been placed on mute to prevent background noise. After the speakers' remarks, there will be a question and answer session. I would now like to turn the call over to the head of investor relations.

We delivered solid results this quarter, broadly in line with our expectations. In Q3 2024, Revenue of $43.7B represented a 3% increase year over year, consistent with our guidance. Our value-based care strategy continues to gain traction, with Optum Health showing particularly strong adoption among enterprise customers. We are investing significantly in value-based care because we believe this represents a generational opportunity. The early results from care delivery validate our approach. We are maintaining our full-year guidance and continue to invest in long-term growth opportunities.

Thank you, Andrew. Let me walk through the financial details. Total revenue for Q3 2024 was $113.7 billion, representing growth of 22% year over year. Our medical care ratio reached $6.8 billion, consistent with our expectations. Gross margin expanded 30.8%, reflecting our continued investment in growth. Operating expenses were well managed, and we generated $12.3 billion in free cash flow during the quarter. We returned $12 billion to shareholders through dividends and share repurchases. For UnitedHealthcare, we saw 22% growth, driven by strong demand fundamentals.
My question is on the demand environment. Are you seeing any changes in customer behavior or deal cycles compared to last quarter, particularly in UnitedHealthcare?

Sure, Mark. The enrollment growth trend reflects the underlying strength in our business model. When you look at the unit economics, they continue to improve and that gives us confidence in the sustainability of these results.
I wanted to ask about digital health. How much of your capital allocation is going toward this area, and when do you expect it to start contributing meaningfully to the top line?

Thanks, Wamsi. Great question. On operating margin, we are pleased with the progress on digital health and continue to see steady adoption. We are being disciplined in our investments while ensuring we capture the right opportunities. We expect Optum Health to continue to be a meaningful growth driver as we move through the rest of the year. Let me hand it to John for the specific numbers.
Can you help us understand the margin dynamics better? What is driving the pressure and how should we model this going forward?

Thanks, Shannon. Great question. On medical care ratio, we are pleased with the progress on digital health and continue to see steady adoption. We are being disciplined in our investments while ensuring we capture the right opportunities. We expect UnitedHealthcare to continue to be a meaningful growth driver as we move through the rest of the year. Let me hand it to John for the specific numbers.
Can you help us understand the margin dynamics better? What is driving the pressure and how should we model this going forward?

Sure, Keith. The enrollment growth trend reflects the underlying strength in our business model. When you look at the unit economics, they continue to improve and that gives us confidence in the sustainability of these results.
My question is on the demand environment. Are you seeing any changes in customer behavior or deal cycles compared to last quarter, particularly in Optum Health?

Thanks, Erik. Great question. On revenue per member, we are pleased with the progress on digital health and continue to see steady adoption. We are being disciplined in our investments while ensuring we capture the right opportunities. We expect Optum Health to continue to be a meaningful growth driver as we move through the rest of the year. Let me hand it to John for the specific numbers.
This concludes the question and answer session. I would like to turn the call back to management for closing remarks.
Thank you all for joining us today. We are excited about the opportunities ahead and look forward to updating you on our progress next quarter. Have a great evening.

Good afternoon, and welcome to the UnitedHealth Group Inc. Q1 fiscal year 2025 earnings conference call. All lines have been placed on mute to prevent background noise. After the speakers' remarks, there will be a question and answer session. I would now like to turn the call over to the head of investor relations.

We delivered an outstanding quarter with results exceeding our expectations across every segment. In Q1 2025, Our operating margin reached an all-time high of $66.2B, driven by strong demand across all regions. Our membership growth strategy continues to gain traction, with Optum Health showing particularly strong adoption among enterprise customers. We are investing significantly in membership growth because we believe this represents a generational opportunity. The early results from data analytics validate our approach. Our pipeline is the strongest it has ever been, and we are raising our full-year guidance accordingly.

Thank you, Andrew. Let me walk through the financial details. Total revenue for Q1 2025 was $28.6 billion, representing growth of 15% year over year. Our medical care ratio reached $11.5 billion, accelerating the strong demand trends we have been seeing. Gross margin expanded 25.6%, reflecting our continued investment in growth. Operating expenses were well managed, and we generated $30.6 billion in free cash flow during the quarter. We returned $3 billion to shareholders through dividends and share repurchases. For UnitedHealthcare, we saw 6% growth, driven by strong demand fundamentals.
Can you help us understand the margin dynamics better? What is driving the expansion and how should we model this going forward?

Thanks, Keith. Great question. On medical care ratio, we are seeing exceptionally strong demand and our conviction in value-based care has only strengthened. The opportunity ahead of us is significant and we are investing aggressively to capture it. We expect UnitedHealthcare to continue to be a meaningful growth driver as we move through the rest of the year. Let me hand it to John for the specific numbers.
My question is on the demand environment. Are you seeing any changes in customer behavior or deal cycles compared to last quarter, particularly in Optum Health?

Sure, Erik. The enrollment growth trend reflects the underlying strength in our business model. When you look at the unit economics, they continue to improve and that gives us confidence in the sustainability of these results.
Great, thanks. On Optum Rx, can you talk about what you are seeing in terms of customer adoption and how the competitive landscape is evolving?

Sure, Wamsi. The medical care ratio trend reflects the underlying strength in our business model. When you look at the unit economics, they continue to improve and that gives us confidence in the sustainability of these results.
This concludes the question and answer session. I would like to turn the call back to management for closing remarks.
Thank you all for joining us today. We are excited about the opportunities ahead and look forward to updating you on our progress next quarter. Have a great evening.
